label: plain_text
text:
Let us see what happens to the trigonometric ratios of angle
A, if it is made smaller and smaller in the right triangle ABC
(see Fig. 8.16), till it becomes zero. As Z A gets smaller and
smaller, the length of the side BC decreases.The point C gets
closer to point B, and finally when Z A becomes very close
to O°, AC becomes almost the same as AB
(see Fig. 8.17).
